notes: v0.2 — reconstructed AI Search tail set with passport_validity cycle-3 starter rows, five promoted-family revalidation rows, and cross-family controls. Rebuilt after v0.1 loss documented in AI_Search_Results/README.md.

passport_validity.prompt: 8 months remaining on passport
passport_validity.note: Number-first word order with 'remaining'; legacy window_patterns require 'passport valid(ity)' adjacency.

passport_validity.prompt: passport with 6 months left
passport_validity.note: 'months left' phrasing absent from legacy patterns.

passport_validity.prompt: passport valid for at least 1 year
passport_validity.note: Legacy catches boolean passport validity but drops the year-unit minimum. Convention: 1 year -> 12 months.

passport_validity.prompt: passport valid for 2 years
passport_validity.note: Legacy catches boolean passport validity but drops the year-unit minimum. Convention: 2 years -> 24 months.

passport_validity.prompt: passport expiring within 3 months should be rejected
passport_validity.note: 'Expiring within X months' is an inverse phrasing the LLM should interpret as 'must have X+ months remaining'. Convention call: human review the boundary.

passport_validity.prompt: freshly renewed passport
passport_validity.note: Renewed/fresh implies valid; no explicit 'valid' keyword.

passport_validity.prompt: newly issued passport
passport_validity.note: Newly issued implies valid; no explicit 'valid' keyword.

passport_validity.prompt: passport not expired
passport_validity.note: Negation of expiry == validity intent. Distinguish from polarity-inversion controls like 'expired passport OK'.

passport_validity.prompt: ensure passport is current
passport_validity.note: Legacy fallback may fire due to 'current' context word but with no 'passport ... valid' adjacency the requested_label may be wrong. Manually verify.

passport_validity.prompt: passport must have minimum 6 months validity remaining
passport_validity.note: Legacy may catch boolean validity but not the months value due to 'minimum' interspersed with 'validity remaining' phrasing.

passport_validity.prompt: candidate should hold a passport that doesn't expire in the next year
passport_validity.note: Conditional expiry phrasing. Convention: 'in the next year' -> 12 months minimum remaining.

passport_validity.prompt: passport remaining validity of 9 months
passport_validity.note: 'remaining validity' phrasing absent from legacy patterns.

passport_validity.prompt: valid US visa and passport
passport_validity.note: Compound prompt: legacy us_visa parser may catch 'US visa' but passport_validity expression is parsed weakly because 'valid' is shared. Multi-family expected; LLM should emit both.

passport_validity.prompt: needs a fresh passport for joining vessel
passport_validity.note: 'Fresh passport' as informal validity. Joining-vessel context shouldn't change family.

passport_validity.prompt: passport should be valid through end of 2028
passport_validity.note: Date-based expiry. Legacy fallback may fire from 'valid' context but cannot compute months remaining (depends on evaluation date). Convention: drop minimum_months_remaining for date-based prompts and surface as date-based hard filter when supported.

passport_validity.prompt: the candidate carries a passport with no validity issues
passport_validity.note: Indirect validity expression; no explicit 'valid' adjacent to 'passport'.

passport_validity.prompt: passport validity 12+ months
passport_validity.note: Compact '12+' notation; legacy patterns expect 'at least' / 'minimum' qualifiers or a months-prefix.

passport_validity.prompt: must have indian passport with valid OCI
passport_validity.note: Legacy parses 'must have...valid passport' family but OCI mention is not a separate family (out of catalog). Expected: passport_validity only, OCI as semantic_query material.

passport_validity.prompt: passport with at least six months till expiry
passport_validity.note: Spelled-out number + 'till expiry' phrasing absent from legacy patterns.

passport_validity.prompt: candidates whose passport has at least 6 months left before it expires
passport_validity.note: Verbose recruiter phrasing. Same intent as canonical 'passport valid for at least 6 months'.

age_range.prompt: officers in their 40s with tanker experience
age_range.note: Decade-span ('in their 40s') absent from legacy patterns. Convention: in their 40s -> [40, 49].
Spelled-out decade. Legacy requires digit age_token; 'thirties' has no digits.
Mid-decade. Convention: mid 40s -> [43, 47].

age_range.prompt: looking for engineers in their late twenties
age_range.note: Late-decade, spelled. Convention: late twenties -> [27, 29].
Early-decade. Convention: early 50s -> [50, 52].
Approximate age. Convention: around N -> [N-2, N+2].
Approximate age. Legacy does not parse this phrasing. Convention: around/approximately N -> [N-2, N+2].

age_range.prompt: no candidate above the age of 60 please
age_range.note: Legacy max_patterns include 'above the age of'; verify polarity.

age_range.prompt: must be at least 25 and no more than 55
age_range.note: Compound min+max without explicit age anchor. Legacy does not parse either side as age.

age_range.prompt: officers older than 30 and younger than 50
age_range.note: Compound strict. Convention: strict older/younger -> exclusive.
Legacy 'under N' fires max_age=35 (legacy treats as inclusive). Convention: under N -> max_age = N-1 (strict).
Hyphen range without 'years old'/'aged' anchor; legacy does not parse this as age.

age_range.prompt: candidates older than 25 but younger than 50
age_range.note: Compound strict on both.
'N plus' notation; not in legacy min_patterns.

age_range.prompt: should be in the 30 to 45 age bracket
age_range.note: Generic age-context fallback should catch via 'age' + two plausible numbers.
'N+' compact notation.
Birth-year phrasing — implies max age = current_year - birth_year. As of 2026: born after 1985 -> max_age 41. Convention call: corpus uses 2026 reference.
Inverse birth-year: born before 1980 -> min_age 46 (2026 ref).

age_range.prompt: officers around the age of 40 to 45
age_range.note: Generic age-context fallback should catch the 40 and 45 numbers; 'around' modifier may make it broader.

age_range.prompt: minimum experience 5 years and age over 30
age_range.note: Compound prompt — sea_service + age. Legacy 'over N' catches age side; sea_service is separate unapplied family.
'aged N to N years' — range_patterns should catch via 'aged' anchor.
Negation phrasing 'not above N'. Legacy misreads this as min_age=51; expected is max_age=50.

us_visa.note: Class name without 'visa' keyword.

us_visa.prompt: must have B one slash B two stamped
us_visa.note: Spelled-out B1/B2 class without visa keyword; legacy does not map this to US visa.
'American visa' uses adjective; _SUPPORTED_VISA_CONTEXT_CUES includes 'american'.
Informal 'Aussie' not in cues (only 'australian'/'mcv'). LLM rescue should map to australia.
Maritime Crew Visa intent without the MCV/visa token; legacy does not map this to Australia visa.
Legacy may catch via window patterns; verify visa_group resolution.
Legacy fires us_visa but normalizes C1-D as C1, dropping the intended C1/D class.

us_visa.prompt: either US or Schengen visa is fine
us_visa.note: Multi-group prompt. Convention: take first stated group; alternative in semantic_query.
L-1 class is not in legacy visa type definitions, even with the visa keyword.

us_visa.prompt: visa for travel to Europe ready
us_visa.note: 'Europe' implies Schengen but cues use 'European'.
Full phrase is in cues; should fire australia group.
Numeric duration with class shorthand.

us_visa.prompt: EU travel visa with 1 year validity
us_visa.note: EU + visa, year unit. Convention: 1 year -> 12 months.
Class shorthand only; no 'visa' word. F-1/O-1 are in cues.
Compact U.S. spelling and 10+ month notation are not parsed by legacy visa patterns.
'Work permit' is non-canonical for visa; legacy expects 'visa'. LLM should interpret as USA visa requirement.

us_visa.prompt: European entry permit holder for crew
us_visa.note: Schengen/Europe travel authorization intent without 'visa' or Schengen keyword; legacy does not map this to Schengen.

us_visa.prompt: C1/D and US visa both required
us_visa.note: Compound mention of C1/D + US visa; legacy may fire on either, accepted_types should include C1/D.
Legacy fires C1/D as US visa but drops the 8-month remaining-validity requirement.

us_visa.prompt: USA H1B holder with 18 months validity
us_visa.note: Class without hyphen + duration.
'Schengen' in cues; 'long-stay' qualifier shouldn't change family.

us_visa.prompt: MV applicants need Maritime Crew Visa
us_visa.note: Critical disambiguation test: 'MV applicants' = vessel prefix, but 'Maritime Crew Visa' explicit. Australia group should fire.

stcw_basic.prompt: candidate completed PSSR, PST, FPFF and EFA
stcw_basic.note: Legacy has lookahead with all four acronyms (line 1879). Should HIT; if so, move to solved-set.

stcw_basic.prompt: holds PSSR, PST, fire prevention and first aid
stcw_basic.note: Spelled component names instead of FPFF/EFA acronyms.
'basic modules' not in patterns (legacy has 'basic courses', 'basic certificates').
'STCW certificate set' is ambiguous — could include advanced. Default unsupported per blueprint §9.3.
Legacy '\bbst\b' fires (line 1864). If solved, move to solved-set.

stcw_basic.prompt: basic safety training completed last month
stcw_basic.note: 'basic safety training' is in patterns. Verify.
A-VI/1 STCW basic code is not parsed by legacy stcw_basic patterns.

stcw_basic.prompt: Personal Survival, Fire Fighting, First Aid, PSSR all valid
stcw_basic.note: Spelled component list — lookahead needs acronyms.
Legacy '\bbst\b' fires.
Legacy 'stcw basic refresher current' fires but 'pending' inverts intent.
'STCW course' too broad — could be advanced. Default unsupported.
Two of four components. Should NOT fire stcw_basic (requires all four).

stcw_basic.prompt: must hold all basic STCW certificates valid
stcw_basic.note: Legacy pattern fires (line 1884).

stcw_basic.prompt: all four basic STCW with original certs
stcw_basic.note: Legacy 'all four basic stcw' fires (line 1874).
Re-ordered component list; lookahead is order-independent.

stcw_basic.prompt: all four basic complete and valid
stcw_basic.note: 'all four basic complete' without 'certificates'/'courses' anchor.
Legacy '\bbst\b' fires regardless of 'refresher in date'; verify polarity.
'STCW 2010 basic' versioned reference; legacy patterns need direct 'STCW basic safety' or 'basic STCW' adjacency.
Parenthetical basic phrasing is not parsed by legacy stcw_basic patterns.
'four-pack' is informal; legacy patterns expect 'four basic' phrasing.
'redoed' (refresher synonym). Convention: implies validity reset, fires stcw_basic.

stcw_basic.prompt: AFF and advanced FA only — no basic
stcw_basic.note: Advanced certs explicitly + 'no basic' negation. Should NOT fire stcw_basic. LLM rescue critical here.

certificate_requirement.note: Advanced Fire Fighting. Separate from stcw_basic.
Spelled out. Convention: 'medical care' -> MFA per catalog.
Advanced DCE distinct from base DCE.
Global Maritime Distress and Safety System.
Triple advanced acronyms. Distinguish from stcw_basic.
Single advanced cert. Tests veto: must NOT classify as stcw_basic.
Proficiency in Survival Craft and Rescue Boats.
Oil-specific tanker familiarization. Convention: cargo-specialized cert.
LMS — STCW Reg II/2 management-level requirement.

rank_match.note: '2/E' slash. Legacy normalize converts to '2 E'; alias must include this.

rank_match.prompt: applied for the position of Chief Engineer

rank_match.note: 'CO' abbreviation may not be in alias table.
Legacy rank alias table does not match bare Cook in this phrasing; reefer remains semantic/vessel context.
Captain/Master synonymous. Convention: collapse to master.

rank_match.prompt: chief galley cook for cruise vessel
rank_match.note: Galley-cook phrasing implies chief_cook but legacy rank aliases do not match it.

rank_match.prompt: applied as pump man on tanker
rank_match.note: Separated 'pump man' phrasing implies pumpman but legacy rank aliases do not match it; tanker may still parse as vessel_type.
Generic junior engineer; may map to specific 4E or stay as junior.
Legacy rank alias table does not match engine_cadet in this phrasing.
AB Special (AB(S)) — rated AB. Maps to able_seaman canonical.
Legacy catches fitter but not motorman in this slash phrasing; expected normalized ranks include both.

controls.prompt: no passport required for this position
controls.note: Polarity inversion: explicit 'no passport required'. Legacy currently fires passport_validity; shadow/active normalizer must veto this.
Figurative 'without passport restrictions' — does not assert passport requirement.
Polarity inversion: opposite of must_be_valid intent.
Wrong sense — 'passport' refers to photo format, not the document. Legacy currently fires passport_validity; shadow/active normalizer must require document-validity context.
Polarity inversion for us_visa: 'visa-free' is the opposite of visa-required. Legacy currently fires us_visa; shadow/active normalizer must veto this.
Explicit visa polarity inversion. Legacy currently fires us_visa; shadow/active normalizer must veto this.
Figurative age expression — must NOT fire age_range. _AGE_FIGURATIVE_PATTERNS covers 'middle-aged'.
'Senior officer' is a rank descriptor, not a numeric age constraint. Should not fire age_range.
Generic 'safety training' without STCW/BST/basic cues — must not fire stcw_basic. _STCW_BASIC_CONTEXT_CUES requires explicit anchor.
'MV' as vessel prefix, NOT a Maritime Crew Visa reference. Should not fire us_visa with visa_group=australia.

controls.prompt: good candidate with stable sea service
controls.note: Qualitative recruiter prompt; should map to semantic_query only, no hard-filter family.

controls.prompt: experienced senior captain who is youthful in approach
controls.note: Two figurative age expressions ('senior captain', 'youthful') — must not fire age_range.
Generic 'certificate' without specific named certificate — must not fire certificate_requirement with empty or invented list.

controls.prompt: candidate must be flexible about rank
controls.note: Anti-constraint on rank — must not fire rank_match. Convention: ignore figurative rank phrasings.
Bare 'documents' without 'passport' keyword — must not fall through legacy passport fallback. Tests the anchor strictness.
